title: Fix SEO meta rewriting on pollinations.ai (run worker on navigations)
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
The `pollinations-ai` worker rewrites per-route SEO tags via HTMLRewriter, but with `compatibility_date >= 2025-04-01` navigation requests are served the static asset directly and skip the worker — so the rewriting never fired (`/play` showed the default `pollinations.ai` title instead of `Play — pollinations.ai`).

- Sets `run_worker_first = true` so the worker runs on all requests; non-HTML responses pass through unchanged.
- Verified on `staging.pollinations.myceli.ai` (with `Sec-Fetch-Mode: navigate`): `/play` → `Play — pollinations.ai`, `/apps` → `Apps — pollinations.ai`, canonical `https://pollinations.ai/play`, og:title + JSON-LD present.

Tradeoff: the worker now runs on every request (incl. static assets), but it only transforms HTML and passes the rest through unchanged; assets stay browser-cached after first load. Can scope with negative globs later if invocation cost matters.

Fixes #11466

gist.headline: Proper introductions
gist.blurb: Fixed a bug where our website pages were forgetting their names. Now, links to specific pages like /play or /apps will show the correct titles and preview cards when you share them!
gist.summary: Set run_worker_first to true in the Cloudflare worker configuration to fix an issue where navigation requests bypassed HTML rewriting for SEO tags.
gist.impact: Route-specific metadata is restored, meaning pages like /play and /apps will now display their correct titles and Open Graph preview cards when shared.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee painting name tags or wooden signposts for different garden paths (representing website routes), while the chunky monitor robot inspects them with a magnifying glass (representing search engines/SEO).
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot painting wooden signposts for different garden paths, with a chunky monitor robot companion holding a magnifying glass to inspect the signs. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and peach gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Nostalgic but beautiful, emotionally warm.
